page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a high-capacity server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. It includes 720 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong performance in cryptographic operations, physics simulations, and software compilation. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only speeds and uncached large-block read/write operations both placing in the top 10%.

The instance is highly capable for demanding workloads such as large-scale Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression, all of which achieve top-tier throughput. Additionally, the presence of Intel AMX enables efficient CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing and text generation on medium to large models. However, single-core CPU performance and standard memory latency benchmarks perform in the average tier, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution may not fully exploit the hardware's scale. This instance is best suited for highly parallelized applications, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a compute-heavy server instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 96 physical cores, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU benchmarks. The processor includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate cryptography, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, the instance excels in memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and high-throughput static web serving. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is optimized for highly parallelized enterprise workloads, database operations, and intensive multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a high-performance instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 features 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count x86_64 compute platform optimized for parallel processing, advanced matrix operations, and high-throughput memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 720 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector math
Excellent throughput for Redis operations and static web serving
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for highly parallelized databases, compilation, and LLM inference
